title: Consolida phrygia
intro: Consolida phrygia, also known as Phrygian poppy or larkspur, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Consolida phrygia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.

side_effects: Consolida phrygia, also known as Phrygian larkspur, is a plant that can be toxic if ingested due to its alkaloid compounds.
